verdicts.corrected_value: Provider of an MCP (modular connector) FX audit server and related payment/FX connector software (appears to publish connector code on GitHub rather than host public API docs).
verdicts.reasoning: The description accurately reflects the content of the iPayX homepage, which discusses the FX forensic audit layer.
While the GitHub repo mentions local configuration, it does not provide any information about iPayX-hosted API authentication methods, making the claim unsupported.
The iPayX homepage is marketing-focused and does not provide developer/API documentation or instructions for obtaining API credentials, confirming the claim of unclear access.
The GitHub repository is the only developer-facing resource and indicates that there is no public API documentation available, confirming the claim of no public API surface.
The lack of vendor-hosted public API documentation and a clear way to obtain API credentials confirms that building an integration against a public vendor API is blocked.
